'''Adam Walker''' (20 February 1991 – 4 October 2022) was a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] {{rlp|pr}}. At club level he played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from Huddersfield at the [[Barrow Raiders]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="WALKER SET FOR GIANTS DEBUT">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/04/01/RUGBYL_Huddersfield.html|title=Walker set for Giants debut DEBUT|publisher=Sportinglife|date=1 April 2010|access-date=1 April 2010}}</ref>

==Background==
Walker was born in [[Bradford, West Yorkshire]], England. He was the twin brother of fellow rugby league player [[Jonathan Walker (rugby league)|Jonathan Walker]].<ref name="sky">{{cite web|title=Super League: Jonathan Walker leaves Castleford to join twin at Hull KR|url=https://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/8955896/super-league-jonathan-walker-leaves-castleford-to-join-twin-at-hull-kr|website=Sky Sports|access-date=24 May 2020|date=4 October 2013}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
Walker started his career at Huddersfield in 2010 where he played sporadically only making 6 appearances for the Giants. He spent most of 2011 on loan at Barrow where he made 16 appearances. In 2012 Walker was sent out on loan again, this time to Swinton where he played 7 times scoring twice.

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Walker was sold to Hull KR in 2013 after claiming that he wanted a new challenge, and also that Hull KR is a big club.<ref name="bbc">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/20236748|title=Hull KR sign Huddersfield Giants prop Adam Walker|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=28 May 2014}}</ref> He scored his first try for Hull KR against London Broncos.<ref name="hulldailymail">{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-KR-Adam-Walker-line-shock-return-London/story-21124814-detail/story.html|title=Hull KR: Adam Walker in line for shock return against London Broncos|work=Hull Daily Mail|access-date=28 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140527220817/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Hull-KR-Adam-Walker-line-shock-return-London/story-21124814-detail/story.html|archive-date=27 May 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> 
[[File:Adam Walker Hull KR.jpg|thumb|right|Walker playing for Hull KR in 2016]]
Walker played 107 times for Hull KR until he rejected a reduced contract when the club was relegated after losing the [[Million Pound Game]] to Salford.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===St Helens===
After departing Hull KR, Walker signed for St Helens for the 2017 season. His time at St Helens was unsuccessful and he failed to see out the end of the season at the club as he was sold to [[Wakefield Trinity]] after making only 9 appearances for the club scoring one try.

===Wakefield Trinity===
After an unsuccessful start to the season at St Helens, Walker signed for Wakefield on a 2½-year deal<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.seriousaboutrl.com/adam-walker-wakefield-6999|title=Wakefield sign Walker from Saints|publisher=Serious about Rugby League|date=25 May 2017|access-date=23 January 2018}}</ref> but only played 7 times before being dismissed when he tested positive for cocaine.<ref>{{cite news |title=Wakefield suspend Adam Walker after positive drug test |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/aug/04/rugby-league-round-up-adam-walker-wakefield |access-date=4 October 2022 |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=4 August 2017 |language=en}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
Walker signed a 2-season contract with Salford commencing at the completion of his drugs ban in March 2019.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bradford-born Adam Walker to resume career at Salford after drugs ban |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/17349004.bradford-born-adam-walker-resume-career-salford-drugs-ban/ |access-date=4 October 2022 |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=10 January 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=The Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019 }}</ref>

On 21 February 2020 it was announced that, by mutual agreement, Walker had left Salford with immediate effect<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/51585406|title=Adam Walker: Salford Red Devils announce prop's exit by mutual agreement|date=21 February 2020|access-date=21 February 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=21 Feb 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Salford part ways with Adam Walker|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-part-ways-with-adam-walker/}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 11 August 2020 it was announced that Walker had signed for the [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=11 Aug 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Adam Walker signes for Leigh Centurions|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/56973/adam-walker-signs-for-leigh-centurions}}</ref> Only three months later, on 17 November 2020 it was announced that Walker had terminated his contract with Leigh with immediate effect.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=17 Nov 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=Adam Walker departs Leigh|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/adam-walker-departs-leigh/}}</ref>

==International career==
In October 2013, Walker was selected to play for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. He made his international début against Tonga in the group stage. Further appearances followed in October and November 2014, as Walker played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] competition. He played in all of Scotland's games and scored a try in their fixture against France.

In October and November 2015, Walker played in the [[2015 European Cup]] competition.

==Personal life and death==
On 27 January 2016, Walker was charged with two offences of attempting to engage in sexual activity with a child, with a court appearance due later in February of that year. He remained available for selection.<ref name="bbcnews">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/uk-england-humber-35487178|title=Hull Kingston Rovers rugby league player faces child sex charges|publisher=BBC News|access-date=3 February 2016}}</ref> All charges were ultimately dismissed.<ref>{{cite news |title=Bradford-born rugby league star Adam Walker speaks of delight as child sex offences are dropped |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/15044801.bradford-born-rugby-league-star-adam-walker-speaks-of-delight-as-child-sex-offences-are-dropped/ |access-date=4 October 2022 |work=[[Bradford Telegraph and Argus]] |date=24 January 2017}}</ref>

In August 2017, Walker was suspended for 20 months after testing positive for cocaine usage during a drugs test.<ref name="Adam Walker: Wakefield Trinity prop suspended for positive cocaine drugs test">{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40834496|title=Adam Walker: Wakefield Trinity prop suspended for positive cocaine drugs test|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=7 August 2017}}</ref>

Walker died on 4 October 2022, at the age of 31.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Former Hull KR prop Adam Walker passes away as tributes pour in |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/adam-walker-passes-away-hull-7662593 |access-date=4 October 2022 |work=HullLive |date=4 October 2022}}</ref>  His death was reported as suicide and he had been suffering from depression and anxiety.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Campbell |first1=James |title=Tragedy of Adam Walker as troubled rugby star's death confirmed |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/news/hull-east-yorkshire-news/tragedy-adam-walker-troubled-rugby-7664281 |access-date=5 October 2022 |work=HullLive |date=4 October 2022}}</ref>
